title: 1. White balance
text:
Skin tone (color) can be greatly affected by
environmental lighting. To compare photos
taken at different times, you need to set up
the white balance correctly. The best way is to
take photo in a completely white room with
light source. Block the natural light as much
as  possible  and  use  the  strobe  as  a  light
source. Ring ﬂash is good enough and if you
have enough space, a small soft box helps to
reduce reﬂection of the skin.
Set  the  white  balance  manually  with  a
50% gray card. If the room condition is the
same all the time, you do not have to re-set
the  white  balance  again.  Including  a  50%
gray card inside the frame is a good idea; by
doing that, you can get a perfectly matching
skin tone even after the shot.
DIGITAL PHOTOGRAPHY TECHNIQUES
IN COSMETIC ACUPUNCTURE
Photos can add a certain amount of objectivity. Digital photography is an
essential  tool  for  cosmetic  acupuncture  practice.  You  can  discuss
treatment  goals  and  problems  with  objective  photos  and  assess  the
treatment result by comparing the before and after treatment photos. It
will be instant (fast) and useful if you set these up properly.
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
~ !
~
